conditions.name: PTSD
conditions.prevalence: Most claimed mental health condition among veterans

secondaries.name: Sleep Apnea
secondaries.relationship: Medical literature establishes that PTSD medications and hyperarousal symptoms disrupt sleep architecture, contributing to obstructive sleep apnea. Studies show veterans with PTSD have significantly higher rates of sleep-disordered breathing.
secondaries.searchQuery: PTSD secondary sleep apnea service connection

secondaries.name: Migraines
secondaries.relationship: Chronic stress and hypervigilance associated with PTSD trigger vascular changes and neurological dysfunction leading to migraine headaches. Research shows PTSD patients have 3x higher migraine prevalence.
secondaries.searchQuery: PTSD secondary migraine headaches service connection

secondaries.name: GERD
secondaries.relationship: PTSD-related anxiety increases gastric acid production and disrupts gastrointestinal motility. Psychiatric medications commonly prescribed for PTSD are also known to relax the lower esophageal sphincter.
secondaries.searchQuery: PTSD secondary GERD gastroesophageal reflux service connection

secondaries.name: Hypertension
secondaries.relationship: Chronic sympathetic nervous system activation from PTSD elevates blood pressure over time. The sustained fight-or-flight response and cortisol dysregulation contribute to cardiovascular disease including hypertension.
secondaries.searchQuery: PTSD secondary hypertension high blood pressure service connection

secondaries.name: Erectile Dysfunction
secondaries.relationship: PTSD medications (SSRIs, SNRIs) commonly cause sexual dysfunction as a side effect. Additionally, PTSD-related anxiety and stress hormones directly impair sexual function.
secondaries.searchQuery: PTSD secondary erectile dysfunction medication side effect service connection

secondaries.name: Depression
secondaries.relationship: Chronic, unrelenting tinnitus causes significant psychological distress. The constant ringing leads to sleep deprivation, social isolation, and difficulty concentrating, all contributing to major depressive disorder.
secondaries.searchQuery: tinnitus secondary depression mental health service connection

secondaries.name: Anxiety Disorder
secondaries.relationship: Persistent tinnitus triggers anxiety through constant hypervigilance to auditory stimuli. Veterans report heightened startle response and inability to find quiet, leading to generalized anxiety disorder.
secondaries.searchQuery: tinnitus secondary anxiety disorder service connection

secondaries.name: Insomnia
secondaries.relationship: Tinnitus directly disrupts sleep onset and maintenance. The perception of ringing is amplified in quiet environments, making it particularly difficult to fall asleep and causing chronic sleep disorders.
secondaries.searchQuery: tinnitus secondary insomnia sleep disorder service connection

secondaries.name: Migraines
secondaries.relationship: Tinnitus and migraines share neurological pathways involving central sensitization. The constant auditory stimulation from tinnitus can trigger or worsen migraine episodes through sensory overload.
secondaries.searchQuery: tinnitus secondary migraine headaches service connection

conditions.name: Lumbar Spine (Low Back)
conditions.prevalence: Among the most common musculoskeletal claims

secondaries.name: Lower Extremity Radiculopathy
secondaries.relationship: Degenerative disc disease and herniated discs in the lumbar spine compress nerve roots, causing radiculopathy (sciatica) with pain, numbness, and weakness radiating into the legs.
secondaries.searchQuery: lumbar spine secondary radiculopathy sciatica lower extremity service connection

secondaries.name: Knee Condition
secondaries.relationship: Altered gait mechanics from chronic low back pain place abnormal stress on the knees. Compensatory weight shifting and limping cause accelerated wear and degenerative joint disease in one or both knees.
secondaries.searchQuery: lumbar spine secondary knee condition altered gait service connection

secondaries.name: Hip Condition
secondaries.relationship: Chronic lumbar spine dysfunction causes compensatory biomechanical changes that transfer stress to the hip joints. Antalgic gait from back pain leads to uneven hip loading and degeneration.
secondaries.searchQuery: lumbar spine secondary hip condition altered gait service connection

secondaries.name: Depression
secondaries.relationship: Chronic pain from lumbar spine conditions is well-established as a cause of major depressive disorder. The inability to perform daily activities, work limitations, and constant pain significantly impact mental health.
secondaries.searchQuery: lumbar spine chronic pain secondary depression mental health service connection

secondaries.name: Cervical Spine Condition
secondaries.relationship: Compensatory postural changes from lumbar spine disability place increased mechanical stress on the cervical spine. Veterans unconsciously alter posture to minimize low back pain, accelerating cervical degeneration.
secondaries.searchQuery: lumbar spine secondary cervical spine compensatory posture service connection

conditions.name: Knee Condition
conditions.prevalence: One of the most commonly rated musculoskeletal disabilities

secondaries.name: Opposite Knee Condition
secondaries.relationship: Favoring the injured knee shifts weight and mechanical stress to the contralateral knee. This compensatory overloading accelerates cartilage breakdown and causes degenerative joint disease in the opposite knee.
secondaries.searchQuery: knee secondary opposite contralateral knee condition altered gait service connection

secondaries.name: Hip Condition
secondaries.relationship: Knee instability and limited range of motion cause compensatory gait changes that redistribute forces through the hip joint. Chronic limping from knee pain accelerates hip joint degeneration.
secondaries.searchQuery: knee secondary hip condition altered gait compensatory service connection

secondaries.name: Lumbar Spine Condition
secondaries.relationship: Antalgic gait from chronic knee pain disrupts normal spinal alignment and places asymmetric loads on the lumbar spine. Compensatory movement patterns lead to disc degeneration and chronic low back pain.
secondaries.searchQuery: knee secondary lumbar spine back condition altered gait service connection

secondaries.name: Ankle Condition
secondaries.relationship: Knee instability and altered biomechanics change how force is transmitted through the ankle joint. Compensatory foot placement patterns and abnormal pronation lead to ankle joint degeneration.
secondaries.searchQuery: knee secondary ankle condition altered gait biomechanics service connection

secondaries.name: Tinnitus
secondaries.relationship: Sensorineural hearing loss and tinnitus frequently co-occur as both result from damage to cochlear hair cells. The brain's attempt to compensate for missing auditory input produces phantom sounds perceived as tinnitus.
secondaries.searchQuery: hearing loss secondary tinnitus cochlear damage service connection

secondaries.name: Depression
secondaries.relationship: Progressive hearing loss leads to social isolation, communication difficulties, and withdrawal from activities. The resulting loneliness and frustration are well-documented causes of depression in veterans.
secondaries.searchQuery: hearing loss secondary depression social isolation service connection

secondaries.name: Anxiety Disorder
secondaries.relationship: Difficulty hearing in social and professional settings causes anticipatory anxiety. Veterans with hearing loss report significant stress about misunderstanding conversations and missing important sounds.
secondaries.searchQuery: hearing loss secondary anxiety disorder service connection

secondaries.name: Vertigo / Vestibular Disorder
secondaries.relationship: Inner ear damage that causes hearing loss often affects the vestibular system as well. Damage to cochlear structures frequently extends to adjacent vestibular organs, causing balance problems and vertigo.
secondaries.searchQuery: hearing loss secondary vertigo vestibular disorder inner ear service connection

conditions.name: Migraines
conditions.prevalence: Common among post-deployment veterans, often linked to TBI

secondaries.name: Depression
secondaries.relationship: Chronic, debilitating migraines significantly impact quality of life and functional capacity. Repeated episodes of prostrating pain, lost workdays, and inability to participate in activities lead to depressive disorders.
secondaries.searchQuery: migraine secondary depression chronic pain service connection

secondaries.name: Insomnia
secondaries.relationship: Migraine pain disrupts normal sleep patterns, and poor sleep quality in turn triggers more migraines. This bidirectional relationship creates a cycle of chronic sleep disruption.
secondaries.searchQuery: migraine secondary insomnia sleep disorder service connection

secondaries.name: Cervical Spine / Neck Tension
secondaries.relationship: Chronic migraines cause persistent cervical muscle tension and spasm. The neurological pathways between migraine and cervical spine dysfunction are well-established, with each condition aggravating the other.
secondaries.searchQuery: migraine secondary cervical spine neck tension service connection

conditions.name: Cervical Spine (Neck)
conditions.prevalence: Common among combat veterans and those with heavy equipment MOS

secondaries.name: Upper Extremity Radiculopathy
secondaries.relationship: Degenerative disc disease and herniated discs in the cervical spine compress nerve roots exiting the spinal column. This causes radiculopathy with pain, numbness, tingling, and weakness in the arms and hands.
secondaries.searchQuery: cervical spine secondary radiculopathy upper extremity nerve compression service connection

secondaries.name: Migraines / Headaches
secondaries.relationship: Cervical spine dysfunction causes cervicogenic headaches through nerve irritation and muscle tension in the neck. Cervical nerve root compression and facet joint dysfunction directly trigger migraine-type headaches.
secondaries.searchQuery: cervical spine secondary migraine headaches cervicogenic service connection

secondaries.name: Shoulder Condition
secondaries.relationship: Cervical spine restrictions alter shoulder biomechanics as the body compensates for limited neck mobility. Nerve compression from cervical disc disease can also cause referred shoulder pain and weakness.
secondaries.searchQuery: cervical spine secondary shoulder condition nerve compression service connection

secondaries.name: Thoracic Outlet Syndrome
secondaries.relationship: Cervical spine degeneration and postural changes compress the neurovascular bundle in the thoracic outlet. Muscle spasm and structural changes in the cervical region contribute to nerve and vessel compression.
secondaries.searchQuery: cervical spine secondary thoracic outlet syndrome nerve compression service connection

conditions.name: Shoulder Condition
conditions.prevalence: Common in combat veterans and physically demanding MOS

secondaries.name: Cervical Spine Condition
secondaries.relationship: Chronic shoulder pain and limited range of motion cause compensatory cervical spine positioning. Guarding and splinting behaviors alter neck mechanics and accelerate cervical degeneration.
secondaries.searchQuery: shoulder secondary cervical spine neck compensatory service connection

secondaries.name: Opposite Shoulder Condition
secondaries.relationship: Favoring the injured shoulder forces overuse of the contralateral shoulder for lifting, carrying, and overhead activities. This compensatory overloading causes accelerated wear and rotator cuff pathology.
secondaries.searchQuery: shoulder secondary opposite contralateral shoulder overuse service connection

secondaries.name: Depression
secondaries.relationship: Chronic shoulder pain limits daily activities, employment capacity, and recreational pursuits. The functional limitations and constant pain are documented contributors to depressive disorders.
secondaries.searchQuery: shoulder chronic pain secondary depression service connection

secondaries.name: Elbow Condition
secondaries.relationship: Shoulder dysfunction alters the mechanics of the entire upper extremity kinetic chain. Compensatory elbow loading during activities that normally engage the shoulder leads to overuse injuries.
secondaries.searchQuery: shoulder secondary elbow condition compensatory upper extremity service connection

conditions.name: Sleep Apnea
conditions.prevalence: Rapidly growing among veteran claims, often linked to PTSD or weight gain from other conditions

secondaries.name: Hypertension
secondaries.relationship: Obstructive sleep apnea causes repeated oxygen desaturation events that trigger sympathetic surges and vascular inflammation. This is one of the strongest evidence-based links -- untreated OSA is a recognized cause of resistant hypertension.
secondaries.searchQuery: sleep apnea secondary hypertension high blood pressure service connection

secondaries.name: Depression
secondaries.relationship: Chronic sleep fragmentation from sleep apnea disrupts neurotransmitter balance and causes daytime fatigue, cognitive impairment, and irritability -- all risk factors for major depressive disorder.
secondaries.searchQuery: sleep apnea secondary depression chronic fatigue service connection

secondaries.name: Heart Disease
secondaries.relationship: Repetitive hypoxia from sleep apnea causes oxidative stress, systemic inflammation, and endothelial dysfunction. These mechanisms are established pathways to coronary artery disease, atrial fibrillation, and heart failure.
secondaries.searchQuery: sleep apnea secondary heart disease cardiovascular service connection

secondaries.name: Stroke / TIA
secondaries.relationship: Sleep apnea increases stroke risk through hypertension, atrial fibrillation, and hypercoagulability. The repeated oxygen desaturation events damage blood vessels and promote clot formation.
secondaries.searchQuery: sleep apnea secondary stroke cerebrovascular service connection

conditions.name: Flat Feet (Pes Planus)
conditions.prevalence: Common service-connected condition from military footwear and marching

secondaries.name: Plantar Fasciitis
secondaries.relationship: Flat feet alter foot biomechanics, causing excessive pronation that stretches and inflames the plantar fascia. The collapsed arch directly increases tension on this connective tissue with every step.
secondaries.searchQuery: flat feet pes planus secondary plantar fasciitis service connection

secondaries.name: Knee Condition
secondaries.relationship: Flat feet cause abnormal lower extremity alignment through excessive pronation. This malalignment increases medial knee stress and accelerates cartilage wear, leading to patellofemoral syndrome and osteoarthritis.
secondaries.searchQuery: flat feet secondary knee condition malalignment service connection

secondaries.name: Ankle Condition
secondaries.relationship: The excessive pronation from flat feet places abnormal stress on ankle ligaments and the tibialis posterior tendon. This leads to chronic ankle instability, tendinopathy, and degenerative changes.
secondaries.searchQuery: flat feet secondary ankle condition instability pronation service connection

secondaries.name: Lumbar Spine Condition
secondaries.relationship: Flat feet disrupt the kinetic chain from ground up, causing leg length discrepancy effects, pelvic tilt, and increased lumbar lordosis. These biomechanical changes create chronic low back pain.
secondaries.searchQuery: flat feet secondary lumbar spine back pain biomechanics service connection

secondaries.name: Shin Splints / Tibial Stress
secondaries.relationship: Flat feet increase tibial torsion and medial tibial stress during weight-bearing activities. The altered mechanics cause chronic medial tibial stress syndrome and may progress to stress fractures.
secondaries.searchQuery: flat feet secondary shin splints tibial stress service connection
